steps.title: Use the exponent rule
steps.content:
A power of $\frac{1}{2}$ means a square root. In general,
$$u^{1/2}=\sqrt{u}$$

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
Here the full base is $147y$, not just one factor. Apply the rule to the entire product:
$$(147y)^{1/2}=\sqrt{147y}$$

steps.title: Compare the choices
steps.content: Choice A keeps $147$ outside the root. Choice B puts only $147$ inside the root. Choice D squares the expression before taking the root. Choice C matches the square root of the full product. The answer is C.
